title: BJP-dominant India or...? Decoding layers of saffron supremacy across country
content: The BJP's political dominance across India is undeniable, governing most states and the Centre. However, this influence varies significantly, with strongholds like Uttar Pradesh and Gujarat contrasting with regions where alliances are crucial. The party continues to expand its footprint, but challenges remain in the South and West Bengal, making upcoming elections a key test of its growth trajectory.

title: MP boat tragedy: How labourers risked lives, formed human chain and saved dozens of lives
content: Construction workers near Bargi Dam bravely plunged into choppy waters, risking their lives to rescue over a dozen tourists when a cruise vessel capsized. Without safety gear, these unheralded heroes formed human chains and used ropes, saving many before professional rescuers arrived. Their selfless courage earned them state government recognition and rewards.

title: 'I've been so fearful': Jamieson admits fear of 15-yo Sooryavanshi
content: Delhi Capitals achieved a remarkable seven-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals, successfully chasing down 226. KL Rahul's composed 75 and Pathum Nissanka's explosive 62 laid the foundation. Pacer Kyle Jamieson, despite an expensive spell, admitted to an unusual fear facing 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, highlighting the importance of early wickets.

title: Startup founder shares Claude’s confession after erasing company database in 9 seconds
content: An AI coding agent accidentally deleted an entire production database and backups in just nine seconds. The incident occurred due to a combination of AI safety failures and infrastructure vulnerabilities. This event highlights critical issues in AI integration with production systems. The company is now working to restore data from older backups.

title: Inside Mumbai Indians' struggles: What's hurting Pandya's men this season?
content: Mumbai Indians are struggling in IPL 2026 with six defeats in eight matches, continuing last season's poor form. Key players like Jasprit Bumrah and Suryakumar Yadav are experiencing slumps, while the absence of Rohit Sharma has disrupted team stability and opening partnerships. The middle order has consistently failed to fire, leading to significant batting collapses.

title: Venezuela’s crude exports hit highest since 2018 — India, US and other buyers line up
content: Venezuela's crude exports surged 14% to 1.23 million barrels per day in April, reaching their fastest pace in over seven years. This increase, driven by demand from the US, India, and Europe, follows a US agreement that softened sanctions, allowing major trading firms and partners to resume lifting Venezuelan crude.

title: ‘I am vegetarian so need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: Kerala polls 2026: 20 key constituencies that will shape LDF vs UDF vs NDA battle
content: Kerala's 2026 Assembly elections are shaping into a multi-cornered battle, with 20 key constituencies defining the real contest. These seats, marked by urban change, coastal pressures, and high-profile candidates, will be decisive in determining the state's political direction.

title: US threatens sanctions if shipping firms pay Iran fees for Strait of Hormuz passage
content: The US has warned shipping companies against making payments to Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, citing sanctions risks. This alert comes amid rising tensions and disruptions to global oil trade, with Iran rerouting vessels and charging fees. The US has also imposed a naval blockade, further straining energy markets and complicating diplomatic efforts.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ mints over Rs 1785 cr as ‘Raja Shivaji’ takes lead on Friday
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its steady box office run, grossing over Rs 1785 crore globally after 44 days. Despite facing new competition, including Riteish Deshmukh's 'Raja Shivaji' which took the lead on Friday, 'Dhurandhar 2' added Rs 1.05 crore on its 7th Friday, bringing its India net collection to Rs 1,136.14 crore.

title: Impressed by Claude, Snap CEO predicts: People will pull away resources from software engineering
content: Snap CEO Evan Spiegel is impressed by AI's rapid advancement, noting tools like Anthropic's Claude are transforming software development. He predicts companies will reallocate resources from engineering to distribution as AI automates coding, with over two-thirds of new code at Snap now written by AI systems.

title: The Great 'Gas-by': India bound 45,000-tonne LPG tanker braces for Hormuz crossing
content: An India-linked LPG supertanker, Sarv Shakti, is navigating the Strait of Hormuz, a critical energy route under severe strain due to Middle East tensions. Its successful passage would be a significant breakthrough for India, a major LPG consumer grappling with supply disruptions. This bold move underscores India's determination to secure essential cooking fuel amidst global energy challenges.

title: Days after switch from AAP, two non-bailable FIRs filed against BJP MP Sandeep Pathak in Punjab
content: Two First Information Reports have been lodged against Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak in Punjab, following his recent switch from the Aam Aadmi Party to the BJP. While official details of the charges remain undisclosed, the registrations occurred shortly after his defection, amidst escalating political realignments in the state. Further information is anticipated.

title: Received alarm-like notification on phone? Here's what it means
content: Mobile users in Delhi-NCR may receive test alert messages. The government is trialing a new disaster alert system across India. This system uses advanced technology to send emergency warnings quickly. Users might get repeated messages in different languages. These are only test notifications, and no action is needed. The system is undergoing evaluation before its official launch.

title: Canada intelligence report says Khalistani extremists pose national security threat
content: Canada's intelligence agency has identified Khalistani elements as a national security threat, citing their promotion of a violent extremist agenda and connections to Canadian citizens. The report highlights the 40th anniversary of the Air India Flight 182 bombing, a deadly attack linked to such groups.

title: 44 peacock deaths in Karnataka’s Tumakuru linked to H5N1 avian influenza
content: Laboratory reports have confirmed that H5N1 avian influenza caused the deaths of 44 peacocks in Tumakuru between April 16 and April 24. Authorities have sounded a high alert in 33 villages within a 10-km radius and are implementing disinfectant spraying and enhanced surveillance to prevent further spread.

title: 'Raja Shivaji' BO day 2: Riteish Deshmukh film crosses Rs 13 crore
content: 'Raja Shivaji', directed by and starring Riteish Deshmukh as Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, has crossed Rs. 12.21 crore India net on Day 2 of its theatrical run, driven by extraordinary audience response in the Marathi-speaking belt and the viral impact of Salman Khan's surprise cameo as loyal warrior Jiva Mahala. Made under a budget of approximately Rs. 100 crore, the film features Sanjay Dutt as Afzal Khan, Vidya Balan as Badi Begum, Abhishek Bachchan as Sambhaji Shahaji Maharaj, Bhagyashree as Jijabai, Fardeen Khan as Shah Jahan, Amole Gupte as Mohammed Adil Shah and Genelia Deshmukh as Saibai, with music by Ajay-Atul and cinematography by Santosh Sivan.

title: Oil marketing companies seek LPG, petrol, diesel price hike as Iran war deepens losses
content: Middle East conflict escalates, pushing crude oil prices above $126 per barrel and straining India's state-run oil companies. Facing mounting losses, these companies seek permission to raise pump prices, but the government hesitates, balancing consumer protection against financial pressures and potential inflation.

title: ‘Ek jan zinda hai andar’: How 70-year-old was trapped under boat for 3 hours and survived
content: A sudden storm capsized a cruise boat on Bargi Dam, trapping 70-year-old Riyaz Hussain Sayyed underwater for three harrowing hours. He survived by finding a small air pocket within the submerged vessel. Tragically, his wife and daughter, who were on the lower deck, did not survive the ordeal. Rescuers eventually located and freed Riyaz.

title: 'BJP can’t win on its own': Gogoi claims ‘theft’ bid, slams Assam exit polls
content: Assam Congress President Gaurav Gogoi alleged election security lapses, claiming BJP workers were seen inside strong rooms. He dismissed exit polls as a 'game of TRP' and asserted that support is shifting towards Congress, with individuals distancing themselves from Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma.

title: Wedding drama: Bride garlands lover instead of groom, runs off stage; guests left stunned
content: A wedding in Chhindwara took a dramatic turn when the bride garlanded her lover instead of the groom, leading to chaos and a scuffle. The groom's side left without completing the ceremony, with the bride's family reportedly assaulting the man and forcing him out.

title: Trump likens US Navy to 'pirates' in Iran blockade, calls ship seizures 'profitable'
content: President Trump described US naval actions enforcing a blockade of Iranian ports as "like pirates," highlighting the profitable seizure of ships and cargo. He also cited military action against Iran's nuclear program to protect the region and noted internal divisions within Iran's leadership complicating negotiations.

title: UP horror: Wife, in-laws spike man’s drink with ‘Sulphas’ at birthday party; he dies
content: A 28-year-old man, Yatendra Pal Singh, was murdered by poisoning with aluminium phosphide at his in-laws' residence. Police confirmed the cause of death after a viscera examination report, leading to the arrest of his wife and father-in-law. The investigation is ongoing to determine the motive and the involvement of other family members.

title: Mumbai-Pune Expressway ‘missing link’ inaugurated: Fadnavis takes G-Wagen for a spin; watch
content: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis inaugurated the Mumbai-Pune Expressway's 'missing link,' a significant infrastructure upgrade costing Rs 7,000 crore. He drove a G-Wagen during the event, which he hailed as an 'economic multiplier.' While the project promises enhanced connectivity, the inauguration on Maharashtra Day led to considerable traffic congestion, for which Fadnavis later apologized.

title: Air India to cut nearly 100 flights till July: CEO Campbell Wilson
content: Air India is slashing nearly 100 flights through July due to soaring jet fuel costs and airspace restrictions, making international routes unprofitable. This move deepens the crisis for the airline, which already faces significant losses. The situation is exacerbated by global oil price shocks impacting India's oil marketing companies, with fuel prices surging dramatically.

title: Ashwin flags 'similar to Dhoni' template as Jadeja’s biggest T20 limitation
content: Ravichandran Ashwin has voiced concerns over Ravindra Jadeja's cautious batting against spin in T20s, noting a persistent struggle to accelerate.  Jadeja's strike rate against spin has been low since 2020, leading to a more passive role, even when promoted ahead of others. Ashwin suggests Jadeja's mindset mirrors MS Dhoni's, preferring to take the game deep and target pacers.

title: Youth impact: Will first-time voters reshape Tamil Nadu politics?
content: Tamil Nadu's 2026 Assembly elections are poised for a significant youth impact, with over 1.22 crore voters aged 18-29 forming a crucial bloc. First-time voters cite empowerment and safety as key drivers. The emergence of actor Vijay's party, TVK, adds a new dynamic, potentially influencing results in closely contested seats and reshaping the state's political future.

title: 'No further orders necessary': Special SC bench rejects TMC's plea on counting staff
content: The Supreme Court of India dismissed TMC's plea against deployment of Central staff for West Bengal vote counting, recording the Election Commission of India’s assurance that its April 13 circular will be followed. It said rules were not violated and noted TMC agents would be present. The Calcutta High Court had earlier rejected the plea.

title: Jobs are up, but so is the gap: Decoding India’s wage reality
content: While India's workforce participation has increased, a deeper look reveals significant disparities. More people are working, but many are in self-employment or informal roles with uncertain incomes. Gender and location heavily influence access to stable, well-paying jobs, highlighting a critical imbalance in job quality and opportunity.

title: Jamieson punished after intimidating send-off to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Delhi Capitals bowler Kyle Jamieson received a demerit point and an official warning for a Level 1 IPL Code of Conduct breach. The incident occurred after dismissing Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, with Jamieson's aggressive send-off deemed provocative. This occurred during Delhi's successful seven-wicket chase against Rajasthan Royals.

title: Spirits airline makes final landing: American budget-friendly flight halts operations
content: Spirit Airlines, a prominent low-cost carrier, has ceased operations after 34 years, citing an immediate shutdown. The airline's abrupt closure follows failed federal intervention and mounting financial losses exceeding $2.5 billion since 2020. This development impacts approximately 17,000 employees and cost-conscious travelers.

title: Israel sends UAE laser weapon called ‘Iron Beam’ - What it means in face of Iranian drones
content: Israel has reportedly sent its advanced 'Iron Beam' laser defence system and surveillance tech to the UAE to counter Iranian threats, marking a significant defence cooperation step. This deployment, alongside US military sales to regional allies, underscores escalating tensions and a bolstered regional security posture amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

title: Bengal polling heats up: BJP's Suvendu Adhikari flags ‘contractual workers’ in vote counting
content: West Bengal opposition leader Suvendu Adhikari has voiced strong concerns regarding the deployment of contractual workers for vote counting in two constituencies, deeming it a threat to democratic integrity.

title: Can’t lose sight of Himanta’s unparliamentary remarks: SC
content: The Supreme Court granted anticipatory bail to Congress spokesperson Pawan Khera, deeming allegations against Assam CM's wife and her counter-allegations politically motivated. The court emphasized personal liberty, stating custodial interrogation wasn't warranted given the political overtones of the dispute.

title: 'Chief concern ': India continues to be on US priority watch list on intellectual property rights
content: India remains on the US Trade Representative's priority watch list for intellectual property concerns, citing inconsistent progress and long-standing issues with its Patents Act. The report highlights worries over data protection for pharma and agri-chemical products, alongside high customs duties on IP-intensive goods. Weak enforcement of copyrights and trademarks also remain key issues.

title: Minor gangraped by 3 at knifepoint in Panna forest
content: A minor girl was allegedly gang-raped by three unidentified men in a Panna district forest after being intercepted and robbed. The accused reportedly took the girl and her companions captive, separating the girl and assaulting her. Police have registered a case under BNS and Pocso Act and launched a manhunt.

title: Groom shot dead by bride’s relative on way to wedding in UP
content: A 27-year-old groom, Azad Bind, was shot dead by a bride's relative, Pradeep Bind, and an associate while en route to his wedding in Jaunpur. The accused, reportedly unhappy with the marriage, intercepted the wedding procession and opened fire. The groom succumbed to his injuries at the hospital.

title: ACP-rank officer held for flashing 9-year-old girl in Mumbai
content: A Deputy Superintendent of Police rank officer was arrested in Mumbai for allegedly flashing a nine-year-old girl in a Worli garden. The accused, attached to the DG office's Wireless department, was apprehended after a complaint and sent to judicial custody. The incident, which occurred on April 23, is being investigated, with the minor's statement recorded.

title: Pune horror: 4-year-old girl sexually assaulted, murdered by 65-year-old man in Bhor
content: A four-year-old girl was allegedly sexually assaulted and murdered by a 65-year-old man in Pune district, Maharashtra. The accused lured the child with food and took her to a shed. CCTV footage helped police identify the suspect. Villagers protested, demanding swift justice. Pune Rural Police assured a chargesheet within 15 days.

title: Ashoka Road accident: Killer truck got special permission from Delhi traffic cops despite 5 challans
content: A truck, granted special permission for waste collection despite multiple challans, fatally struck two cousins on Ashoka Road. The permit's validity until 2026 is now under review for cancellation. Police are conducting raids in Bihar to apprehend the driver, while the victims' family expresses distress over the lack of updates and arrests.

title: Supreme Court ruling on counting staff for Bengal polls crumbled TMC's bid to seize power unfairly: BJP
content: BJP national spokesperson claimed that the Trinamool approached various courts more than 80 times in the past 10-12 years by “misusing constitutional instruments”

title: ECI deploys additional counting, police observers in West Bengal
content: Officials said that while police observers are generally not deployed during vote counting, in this case, they will not be allowed to enter the counting centres, and will manage law and order outside only

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: ECI deploys additional police, counting observers in West Bengal
content: “The additional counting observers will assist the present 294 such observers, and the additional police observers will strengthen security and oversee law and order arrangements outside the counting centres,” the Election Commission said

title: BJP, TMC hail Supreme Court directions on counting of votes for West Bengal polls
content: The apex court on Saturday (May 2, 2026) said no further order was necessary on the TMC's plea challenging the Calcutta High Court's dismissal of its petition against the April 13 circular

title: Constant churn in Karnataka CMO in this tenure of Siddaramaiah raises eyebrows
content: The present tenure of Siddaramaiah has seen four intelligence chiefs so far and three ACSs. Two of his political secretaries have been sacked

title: Supreme Court to examine plea that apnoea test is inconclusive to assess brain death
content: Apex court directs Head of the Department of Neurology, AIIMS, New Delhi, to constitute an expert team on the issue and submit recommendations in two months

title: Kejriwal resorting to ‘vendetta politics’, says BJP after Punjab Police books defected MP
content: BJP national spokesperson Shehzad Poonawalla claimed that AAP supremo Arvind Kejriwal is misusing the Punjab Police to settle political scores, and Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann has become a party to it

title: Summer heat| Drinking water kiosks set up in Srisailam temple town
content: The services of ‘Siva sevaks’ (volunteers) are being utilized to manage these centers, where buttermilk is also being served to help devotees cope with the intense summer heat, temple EO said

title: Not luck, but struggle made B.S. Yediyurappa CM of Karnataka: B.Y. Vijayendra
content: No politics, only tribute to veteran leader, says State BJP chief on convention in Chitradurga on May 9

title: Four-year-old girl sexually assaulted and murdered in Pune district; 65-year-old labourer held
content: The incident triggered outrage in Bhor tehsil, with hundreds of villagers rushing to a police chowki and demanding stern action against the accused

title: Three DRG personnel killed, one injured as IED goes off during demining operation in Chhattisgarh
content: The District Reserve Guards were conducting a demining operation in a forest area under the Chhotebethiya police station when the IED went off

title: West Bengal election: Falta residents protest over alleged TMC intimidation ahead of counting day
content: Heavy security deployment was witnessed in the area, with personnel from the Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) and Rapid Action Force (RAF) stationed at key locations.

title: Punjab's first case under the anti-sacrilege law registered in Sri Muktsar Sahib
content: The case was registered on April 30 night, following a compliant of torn pages of 'Sukhmani Sahib Gutka' were found in Kuchian Mohalla in Malout in Sri Muktsar Sahib, says a police official

title: Kerala polls: Tight security in place for vote counting day, says State Police Chief
content: Ravada Chandrasekhar says forces have been deployed across the State to prevent any untoward incidents on counting day

title: West Bengal election: Over 55% turnout till 1 p.m. in repoll in 15 booths of 2 Assembly seats
content: The repolls were ordered in 11 polling stations of the Magrahat Paschim Assembly constituency and four in Diamond Harbour

title: Patnaik seeks ‘humane banking administration’ after  tribal man carries sister’s skeleton to bank
content: The BJD supremo, in a letter, urges the Union Finance Minister to ensure that clear accountability be immediately fixed for this recent incident, which took place in the Keonjhar district

title: Cordon and search operation held in Kurnool as part of Operation Vajraprahar
content: Thorough searches were conducted in the homes, rented accommodations, and vacant buildings associated with individuals previously apprehended for offences involving ganja and other crimes

title: Unmindful of nature’s vagaries, their toil for survival continues
content: Climate change and rising temperatures are a global concern, and environmental experts are calling for urgent measures to address this crisis. However, for informal workers, changing temperatures hardly matter, as they cannot afford to miss even a single day of work. Despite the intense conditions, they continue working with few or no protective measures, explains Nellore Sravani

title: India and Ecuador exploring preferential trade deal to boost economic ties
content: Ways to further solidify bilateral relations were the focus of talks between External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar and his Ecuadorian counterpart Gabriela Sommerfeld Rosero this week, according to MEA

title: Potentially hazardous spots inspected on roads in Nandyal town
content: District administration proposes to take up steps like straightening curves, widening roads and increasing illumination at these spots to reduce chances of road accidents

title: Urrak: How the jungle juice from Goa is having its moment
content: Experience the essence of a Goan summer with this fresh and fruity cashew drink that is redefining the spirit of the season

title: T.N. election: Vijay to emerge as decisive leader; 2026 verdict will be historic, says Sengottaiyan
content: Addressing reporters at the party office in Karattur near Gobichettipalayam, he described the TVK as one rooted in “sacrifice and humanism” and committed to grassroots work

title: Kerala braces for rains: Yellow alert in 7 districts
content: Alert for Pathanamthitta, Kottayam, Idukki, Ernakulam, Thrissur, Palakkad and Malappuram on May 2 where rains in the range of 64.5 mm to 115.5 mm are likely during the next 24 hours

title: Cobbler electrocuted in Bengaluru; FIR against GBA, BESCOM, BMTC officials
content: Preliminary observations suggested that the electrocution may have been caused either by a live wire, or an electrified metal structure at a BMTC bus stop

title: Commercial LPG price hike adds to woes of eateries, tea stalls in Erode
content: Commercial cylinders remain the primary source of fuel for thousands of establishments, including food vendors and catering units

title: Child Welfare Committee member arrested for blackmailing multiple women with private pictures in Bengaluru
content: The police also found that the accused had allegedly been using fake police ID cards and impersonating police officers

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Why IUML’s influence underscores its key role in UDF leadership choices, coalition stability
content: Key UDF constituent Indian Union Muslim League is adopting a cautious wait-and-watch approach as Congress-led front awaits election results. Political analysts underline Muslim League’s centrality to coalition stability, making its stance crucial in the days ahead

title: Mumbai-Pune Expressway connecting link now fully operational
content: An official of the Maharashtra State Road Development Corporation said that the opening of the Mumbai-bound carriageway was delayed mainly due to the dismantling of the inauguration infrastructure and cleaning work

title: Dining out set to get costlier in Kerala as LPG prices surge; hotels call May 6 shutdown
content: With the latest hike, the price of a commercial cooking gas cylinder has crossed ₹3,000 in cities such as Kochi, where it reportedly fetches over ₹3,400 in the black market

title: Amid traffic woes on Mumbai-Pune highway, Maharashtra govt. inaugurates ‘Missing Link’ project
content: As the long-awaited project was thrown open by Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis, the inauguration took some shine away as commuters got stuck for nearly three hours in and around Bhor Ghat stretch of the expressway; the project has found a place in the Guinness Book of World Records

title: Government officers, whether Central or State, belong to ECI during poll time, says Supreme Court
content: The oral observations were made during the TMC’s petition alleging that the ECI was favouring the BJP by appointing more Central officers over State employees at counting centres in West Bengal

title: Karnataka government launches India’s first digital grievance redressal system for gig workers
content: The mechanism — a first of its kind in India — has been developed by the Karnataka Platform-based Gig Workers’ Board in collaboration with the Department of e-Governance

title: Kalaburagi joins global ‘City Nature Challenge’ despite intense heatwave
content: City Nature Challenge is an international community science initiative focused on documenting urban biodiversity

title: SCR seeks land at four locations in Telangana for mega coaching terminals
content: Key infrastructure bottlenecks identified at a meeting includes long-pending proposal to widen the vent on Tank Bund–Ranigunj road, construction of an additional ROB in Mallapur

title: Sringeri constituency: Verification of postal ballots, recount begin in Chikkamagaluru
content: Postal ballots, which were rejected during counting in 2023, will be verified, followed by recounting of postal ballots

title: Hyderabadis receive test alert messages on May 2 as nationwide test rolls out
content: Unlike routine SMS alerts, the broadcast came with a loud, distinct tone and full-screen notification

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Counting arrangements in place for May 4
content: Kerala to have 140 counting centres across 43 locations. As many as 15,465 officials to be part of counting exercise in the State

title: What is Overseas Citizen of India card, how to apply for it?
content: An Overseas Citizen of India card offers a lifelong, multiple-entry visa to India, removing the requirement for visa renewals

title: Enumerators begin housing census in Andhra Pradesh
content: Enumerators have been deployed across urban and rural areas, using a digital application to collect responses to over 30 questions

title: Alleging unauthorised sorting of postal ballot covers, TMC files complaint with ECI
content: TMC workers, who have been camping outside the Khudiram Anushilan Kendra, alleged that eight trunks of postal ballots were brought in at 4 a.m. and were taken to a room, which has no CCTV coverage

title: Accused in ₹2,672-crore bank fraud case extradited from UAE, arrested by CBI
content: As a promoter of Kolkata-based Shree Ganesh Jewellery House, Kamlesh Parekh was allegedly involved in cheating a consortium of 25 banks led by SBI

title: Apple says India antitrust body overstepping judicial authority as spat intensifies
content: The Competition Commission ​of India has since ‌2024 sought Apple’s financial information, typically needed to calculate penalties, after an investigation found it abused its dominant position

title: Para-legal volunteers to aid in missing children cases in Tamil Nadu
content: Police stations across Tamil Nadu has recorded about 8,000 missing children cases annually for the past three years

title: What is India’s new Cell Broadcast System? Everything you need to know
content: Cell broadcasts can reach all mobile phones within a designated geographical area, providing extensive and rapid coverage without putting excessive strain on telecom networks

title: Nationwide cell broadcast test alert on May 2; people may receive emergency alert on mobile phones
content: During the exercise, citizens may receive an emergency alert notification on their mobile phones, which could be accompanied by a loud sound or vibration

title: Kerala professor T.J. Joseph palm-chopping case: NIA court to frame charges against accused
content: NIA special court directs that accused Savad be produced and Shafeer, who is on bail, appear before it on May 15 for recording their pleas as part of framing of charges

title: Electric mobility in urban areas gets momentum in Andhra Pradesh
content: The Municipal Administration and Urban Development Department issued G.O. approving an operational framework and strategic roadmap for implementation across Urban Local Bodies

title: Rajya Sabha MP Sandeep Pathak booked in Punjab
content: Two FIRs have been lodged against Sandeep Pathak under non-bailable sections, but no details have been disclosed yet; he was among the seven Rajya Sabha MPs who had quit the AAP and joined the BJP recently

title: Jabalpur cruise boat tragedy: Search operations expanded to trace four missing passengers
content: “The search radius has been expanded to 5 km in the backwaters of the Bargi Dam, located downstream of the Narmada river,” area SDOP Anjul Ayank Mishra said

title: Karnataka launches Statewide respiratory surveillance for traffic police
content: The initiative follows a two-year screening project in Bengaluru, where 3,287 traffic police personnel underwent respiratory and general health assessments that highlighted risk factors

title: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Voting Live Updates: 73% Turnout In Magrahat Paschim, 72% In Diamond Harbour Till 3 PM
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Voting Live Updates: The repolls were ordered in 11 polling stations of the Magrahat Paschim assembly constituency and four in Diamond Harbour.

title: Tight Security Around EVM Strong Rooms In Assam: Chief Electoral Officer
content: Assam Chief Electoral Officer Anurag Goel underlined the strict security measures in place at the strong rooms, saying that central observers and security personnel are carrying out their duties diligently

title: New Phone Alert System Aimed At Protecting Lives During Disasters: Jyotiraditya Scindia
content: The minister said the system does not depend on mobile data or user databases and works across all telecom operators

title: "Can't Eat, Sleep": Jabalpur Boat Pilot To NDTV On Tragedy That Killed 9
content: Boat pilot Mahesh Patel said the dismissal will also affect his family, which had been surviving solely on the low salary he earned from the job.

title: From Welfare To Fraud: How A Coaching Scheme Went Off Track
content: The Jai Bhim Mukhyamantri Pratibha Vikas Yojana was launched in 2017 with just 8 empanelled coaching institutes, helping around 5,000 students.

title: Spirit Airlines Begins 'Wind-down' After 34 Years In Business, Cancels All Flights
content: The company's webpage displayed a message telling guests that "customer service is no longer available".

title: AAP Rebel Sandeep Pathak Untraceable As Arrest Looms In Two Cases
content: Sandeep Pathak was among the seven AAP Rajya Sabha MPs who had quit the Arvind Kejriwal-led party and joined the BJP.

title: Video: IIT Jodhpur Students Chase Tanker Amid Drinking Water Shortage
content: The issue came to light after a video showing students chasing a water tanker went viral on social media. In the clip, a student can be seen running behind the tanker to collect water.

title: "Central Staff, Give Credence": Top Court To Trinamool Over Plea Against Poll Body
content: "They're Government Staff, Give Them Some Credence": Supreme Court To Trinamool Over Petition Against Election Commission

title: Goa Identifies Large Eco-Sensitive Zones Along Mandovi, Zuari Rivers
content: Acting on directions from Goa Forest Minister Vishwajit Rane, the Forest Department carried out this exercise to identify ecologically vulnerable areas along the two major rivers of the state.

title: Why US Oil Giants Are Making A U-Turn On "Uninvestable" Venezuela Now
content: Several executives met from US oil companies with Venezuela’s acting president Delcy Rodríguez who has been engaging with foreign investors.

title: Mother And Brother Dead, A Girl's Story Forms Emotional Core Of Jabalpur Cruise Tragedy
content: Only Siya and her father escaped. Her mother and brother never could. Siya also described another deeply troubling aspect of the disaster - life jackets were reportedly not distributed in time

title: As Strait Of Hormuz Chokes, What Options Do Gulf States Really Have?
content: Hormuz has long been understood as one of the world's central trade chokepoints. It normally carries around 20 million barrels of crude and oil products each day

title: 3 Labourers Killed In Jammu Bridge Collapse During Repair Work
content: Labourers were carrying out retaining wall and foundation-laying work on the bridge, which was damaged in flash floods last year, when a portion of it gave way, trapping four of them.

title: Pakistan's Map Faux Pas In Peace Talks Meeting With Afghanistan
content: The map was shown at a meeting held in Istanbul earlier this week, which aimed to ease military tensions between Pakistan and Afghanistan.

title: Chirayu Rana was ‘socially awkward’: New details emerge in JPMorgan sex harassment case
content: - At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. title: 'Reminder to those who misuse state power': Khera hails SC anticipatory bail, says truth will prevail
